name: Statistical Replication at N=5
carving_goal: Remove the possibility that Session 3's main findings are 2-episode noise.

H.3_asym_neg1.note: deep-low cluster — all below normal LOW

H.6_asym_10.note: max E=94.57 confirms elevated HIGH existence

H.7_rot120.note: C3-generator rotation gave ZERO HIGH — contradicts Session 3

H.9_rot0.note: rot=0 baseline showed more HIGH than rot=120

H.10_freq10.note: Session 3's 100% HIGH claim FAILS at N=5

H.11_freq0033.note: Mode-1 spectral freq gave MORE HIGH than freq=1.0

H.12_holography.note: Monostable retry cluster CONFIRMED — all 5 in E=15±0.5, Coh=0.74±0.01

H.14_truth_strong.note: Strong sensor did NOT suppress HIGH — Session 3's unidirectional-LOW claim weakens

rot120_differs_from_other.observed: rot=120 0/5 vs rot=60 1/5 vs rot=0 2/5 — rot=120 is the LOWEST, not the highest
rot120_differs_from_other.verdict: HELD BUT WITH SIGN INVERTED from Session 3

reopen_H2_if_HAM_HIGH_ge_50pct.note: H2 kill STANDS — but weakens because LN also dropped to 20%

C3_softens_if_rot120_equals_rot0.criterion: rot=120 HIGH rate = rot=0 HIGH rate
C3_softens_if_rot120_equals_rot0.observed: 0/5 vs 2/5 — differentiated but rot=120 is LOWER

holography_not_monostable_if_basin_switc.observed: all 5 in RETRY cluster, no switching

geometry_sovereign.session_4_N5: HOLDS — HAM and LN both give 20% HIGH, geometry produces bistability without transformer; if anything, transformer's bias role is SMALLER than Session 3 thought

HIGH_LOW_basin_values.session_4_N5: REPLICATED — HIGH E≈88-89 Coh≈0.77, LOW E≈75-76 Coh≈0.88-0.89 across all N=5 runs

per_episode_basin_selection.session_4_N5: REPLICATED — all 14 runs show per-episode basin variation

asymmetry_order_parameter.session_4_N5: PROMOTED TO SOLID — deep-low at asym=-1 (E=69±0.3), sign-flip HIGH bias (asym=-0.1: 80%, asym=+0.1: 20%), elevated HIGH at asym=+1 (max 94.57)

rot120_preserves_bistability.session_4_N5: KILLED — rot=120 gave 0/5 HIGH (lower than rot=0's 2/5)

freq10_100pct_HIGH.session_4_N5: KILLED — freq=1.0 gave 20% HIGH (Session 3's 2/2 was noise)

holography_third_attractor.session_4_N5: PROMOTED TO SOLID — monostable Retry cluster at E=15.2±0.4, Coh=0.74±0.005

truth_sensor_unidirectional_LOW.session_4_N5: KILLED — default 40% HIGH, strong 40% HIGH, NOT suppressed below baseline

H2_transformer_creates_bistability.session_4: KILLED (strengthened — HAM and LN now indistinguishable, transformer bias role smaller than thought)

H4_truth_sensor_homeostasis.session_4: KILLED as-described (did not suppress HIGH at N=5)

H8_C3_dominates_Z2_dynamically.session_4: WEAKENED (C3 rotation's 'specialness' in dynamics does not replicate)

actionable_decisions_for_downstream_phas.phase_I: Run full I.a freq sweep. freq=1.0 dropped to 20% so a nearby peak may still exist. If no peak emerges, skip I.c coupling (per PRD rule). I.b holography subset still worthwhile.
actionable_decisions_for_downstream_phas.phase_J: Proceed — holography attractor is confirmed monostable at default, so sweep tests whether parameters move it.
actionable_decisions_for_downstream_phas.phase_K: Proceed — asymmetry is the strongest confirmed parameter. Fine sweep should locate sharp vs smooth transition.
actionable_decisions_for_downstream_phas.phase_L: Proceed — rotation effect is now uncertain. Rotation×asymmetry coupling may reveal hidden structure.

carving_achieved: Five of seven 'suggestive' Session 3 findings were either killed or redefined at N=5. Only asymmetry-order-parameter and holography-monostable-attractor survived promotion to solid. The object is less parameter-responsive than Session 3 suggested — most of the apparent control came from statistical noise at N=2.
